name: HMS MTB 678 (MTB 678)
events: 21 Mar 1944 MTB 678 and MTB 676 (Force 112) sailed from Plymouth at 1640 B/20 for an anti-shipping sweep between Lest Roches Douvres and Barnouic. They were joined by MTB 96, MTB 90 and MTB 98 at 1830 B/20 in position 184° - Start Point 19 miles. MTB 98 returned to harbour around 2100 B/20 after having broken down. At about 0200 B/21, MTB 678 and MTB 676 which were to the northward of MTB 96 and MTB 90, obtained radar contact with an enemy force to the southward. They were immediately illuminated and came under very heavy fire and were forced to retire, but without casualties. MTB 96 and MTB 90, to the southward were unable to make contact with the enemy. Force 112 returned to Dartmouth at 0845 B/21.

The enemy encountered was a convoy made up of the German naval tanker Bromberg (former Norwegian Beaumont, 5702 GRT, built 1929) escorted by the minesweepers M 263, M 275, M 366 and M 367. This convoy was on passage from Lorient to Cherbourg. [No further details available for the moment as our (bad) copy of the KTB (log) of the 10th German M-Flotilla is very difficult (impossible) to read.] (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
